home.subtitle: Sana is a cybersecurity awareness platform that finds vulnerabilities in your digital literacy.

protection.title: How Sana protects you
protection.subtitle: A comprehensive set of tools that help test, educate and strengthen digital habits against modern social engineering techniques.

item1.title: Phishing recognition training
item1.titlePlaceholder: e.g., How to recognize WhatsApp fraud
item1.description: Work with highly realistic copies of popular Kazakhstan banking and service portals to safely check your vigilance.

item2.title: Device vulnerability check
item2.description: Interactive lessons on protecting your smartphone, setting up 2FA and recognizing malicious apps masquerading as useful tools.

visual.title: Real-time threat analysis
visual.description: Our AI system continuously monitors and analyzes new fraud schemes worldwide, instantly adapting simulations to protect you from tomorrow's threats today.

home.scamBlocked: Urgent: Your account is blocked! Pay to unlock immediately.

simulation.title: Practice
simulation.hubSubtitle: Test yourself on community scenarios, in an AI test or in random mode to see how well you recognize modern threats.
simulation.learningTracksSubtitle: Select a topic and take a short practice focused on your weak points.
simulation.questionsLimitHint: You can submit up to 20 questions per test.
simulation.sharedFeedbackPlaceholder: General explanation if a specific option doesn't have its own comment.
simulation.sharedModeHint: In this mode, the explanation is entered only once.
simulation.detailedModeHint: If the general comment is empty, fill in the explanation for each option.
simulation.needFeedback: You must fill in either a general comment or an explanation for each option.
simulation.noCommunityScenarios: No community scenarios yet. Be the first to create one!
simulation.reviewNotice: Your scenario will be reviewed by an AI moderator. Make sure one option is clearly the correct action.
simulation.feedbackOptionalHint: Optional. If left blank, shared feedback above will be used.
simulation.needCorrectAnswer: Each question must have at least one correct answer.
simulation.questionsTooMany: Cannot submit more than 20 questions per test.
simulation.aiError: Failed to generate scenario. Try choosing a ready-made topic.
simulation.alwaysVigilant: Always stay alert. Cybercriminals are constantly changing their methods.
simulation.realSituationConsequence: In a real situation, such a choice could lead to financial loss, identity theft, or emotional harm.
simulation.factFallbackNote: If the AI is busy, Sana instantly uses verified facts from the database.
simulation.generatingDesc: Creating a real-world cybersecurity dilemma specifically for you.
simulation.learningCoachExcellent: Great job. You safely handled all scenarios.

articles.noResults: No articles found matching your query.
articles.subtitle: Expand your cybersecurity knowledge with short articles.
articles.learningSubtitle: Open a module, study key signals and launch practice on the topic.
articles.createDesc: Share your cybersecurity knowledge with the community.
articles.titlePlaceholder: e.g., How to recognize WhatsApp fraud
articles.descriptionPlaceholder: Short description for the preview card...
articles.formattingHint: Select text and use buttons to insert HTML formatting.
articles.practicePrompt: Launch a simulation based on this article and earn points.

art1.title: How to spot a phishing email
art1.desc: Learn core markers of malicious emails and trick techniques.
art1.p1: Check the sender domain carefully: letter swaps often reveal fakes.
art1.p2: Urgency and account lock threats are common pressure tactics.
art1.p3: Never enter passwords via email links: open the site manually.

art2.title: Power of 2FA
art2.desc: Secure your accounts with Two-Factor Authentication.
art2.p1: Use an authenticator app instead of SMS where possible.
art2.p2: Save 2FA recovery codes in an offline location.
art2.p3: Enable 2FA for email first: it's the key to other accounts.

art3.title: Public Wi-Fi risks
art3.desc: Why you should never check your bank account in a cafe.
art3.p1: Avoid banking and email login on open networks without VPN.
art3.p2: Verify network names with staff to avoid evil-twin hotspots.
art3.p3: Disable auto-join Wi-Fi on smartphone and laptop.

art4.title: Social engineering basics
art4.desc: Hackers use human psychology. Here is what you need to notice.
art4.p1: If someone asks for secret data under urgency, stop and double check.
art4.p2: Verify the speaker's identity via an independent communication channel.
art4.p3: Never confirm codes or payments if you didn't initiate them.
